Content_Sketch: Suburban living room. TV showing capital city burning. You sit on the sofa.
Illustration_Note: Stickman on sofa facing TV. TV shows orange flames and silhouettes of buildings. Blue TV glow on face. Maryland suburban interior.

Content_Sketch: Your country is being bombed. You are not there. You have not been there since you were seventeen.
Illustration_Note: TV close-up showing missile footage. Sofa in foreground. Distance emphasized.

Content_Sketch: You lost a country before you understood what it was.
Illustration_Note: Weight line. Dark screen. Single stickman silhouette against TV glow.

Content_Sketch: Born in the imperial palace. Crown prince at seven. Rose gardens with guards who know your name.
Illustration_Note: Small stickman in miniature military uniform on palace balcony. Gold tones. Rose gardens below. Guards standing at attention.

Content_Sketch: Ceremonies. Miniature uniforms. Waving from balconies. Your father rules a 2500-year dynasty  -  you are the next link.
Illustration_Note: Palace interior. Father on ornate throne. Small stickman beside him. Gold and red tones.

Content_Sketch: Seventeen. Sent abroad for pilot training. You leave the country for the first time.
Illustration_Note: Young stickman in cadet uniform at airport or airbase. Plane in background. Looking back.

Content_Sketch: The revolution happens while you are on the other side of the ocean. The palace becomes a museum of your family's crimes.
Illustration_Note: Split: left  -  stickman at foreign airbase watching TV. Right  -  palace overrun by crowd of faceless figures.

Content_Sketch: You cannot go back. No one can go back.
Illustration_Note: Stickman standing alone at airbase fence. Looking at horizon. Grey tones.

Content_Sketch: Twenty. Your father dies in a borrowed palace in a borrowed city. Cancer he hid from the family.
Illustration_Note: Hospital bed in borrowed palace. Father stickman lying down. Young stickman standing beside. Muted gold fading to grey.

Content_Sketch: You declare yourself successor  -  to a throne that no longer exists  -  in a rented living room.
Illustration_Note: Young stickman standing alone in small apartment. No throne. No palace. Just a rented room.

Content_Sketch: Your youngest sister dies in a London hotel room. Thirty-one. Overdose. The exile found the crack in her first.
Illustration_Note: Hotel room door. Dim lighting. Single stickman silhouette. London skyline through window.

Content_Sketch: Your brother dies by suicide. Forty-four. The male line of succession ends with him.
Illustration_Note: Empty chair. Boston winter through window. Photograph on mantle.

Content_Sketch: Exile doesn't kill with bullets. It kills with distance.
Illustration_Note: Weight line. Three ghost silhouettes fading: father, sister, brother.

Content_Sketch: Economic protests erupt back home. For the first time in forty years  -  they chant the name of the old dynasty.
Illustration_Note: TV screen showing faceless protesters with arms raised. Stickman watching from sofa.

Content_Sketch: 2022. Women cut their hair in the street. The largest uprising in a generation.
Illustration_Note: TV montage: women cutting hair, crowds marching. Stickman leaning forward on sofa.

Content_Sketch: Your name is chanted in streets you haven't walked since you were seven.
Illustration_Note: Phone screen showing protest video. Stickman holding phone. Hand trembling.

Content_Sketch: They call your name as if you could save them.
Illustration_Note: Weight line. Protest audio visual. Stickman alone in Maryland living room.

Content_Sketch: The regime strikes back. Hundreds arrested. Executions. The streets go quiet.
Illustration_Note: TV showing empty streets. Military vehicles. Stickman watching with clenched jaw.

Content_Sketch: You record videos from an American studio. 4K camera. Professional microphone. Old flag backdrop.
Illustration_Note: Stickman sitting in studio chair. Professional lighting. Old flag behind. Speaking to camera.

Content_Sketch: The internet is cut. No one in the country can watch what you just recorded.
Illustration_Note: Studio screen showing 'connection failed' or buffering. Video going nowhere.

Content_Sketch: You meet Western politicians. Handshakes. Photo ops. Promises. Nothing changes.
Illustration_Note: Stickman shaking hands with faceless suits at podium. Camera flashes. Empty promises.

Content_Sketch: You speak at a conservative forum in America. Standing ovation. Back home they call you a Western puppet.
Illustration_Note: Split: left  -  stickman at podium with applauding crowd. Right  -  TV screen in homeland showing his face with angry caption.

Content_Sketch: The exiled opposition splits. The republicans hate you for the title. The monarchists hate you for refusing the throne.
Illustration_Note: Conference table. Multiple faceless figures arguing. Stickman at head of table  -  caught between.

Content_Sketch: Late night. Your wife finds you alone in the dark living room. TV on mute showing protest footage.
Illustration_Note: Dark living room. Blue TV glow. Stickman on sofa. Wife silhouette in doorway.

Content_Sketch: You know exactly what is needed: a unified coalition. A national strike. International pressure.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk with papers. Strategic diagrams. Clear thinking.

Content_Sketch: You know exactly what you don't have: no army. No territory. No real power.
Illustration_Note: Same desk. Papers scattered. The clarity dissolves into helplessness.

Content_Sketch: You are a symbol of freedom for a nation. And you cannot open your front door without checking the security camera.
Illustration_Note: Front door. Security camera screen. Stickman looking at monitor before opening door.

Content_Sketch: February 2026. American and allied missiles hit the capital for the first time.
Illustration_Note: TV showing missile strikes. Same city where stickman was born. Night sky lit by explosions.

Content_Sketch: You watch it live on cable news. The same city where you were born. The same streets you rode through in an armored car as a child.
Illustration_Note: CNN-style broadcast. Split screen: palace area then vs now. Stickman inches from TV screen.

Content_Sketch: You call it humanitarian intervention. On American television. In English.
Illustration_Note: TV studio. Stickman at interview desk. American flag in background. English text lower third.

Content_Sketch: You ask them to spare the infrastructure. That belongs to my people. Not the regime.
Illustration_Note: Stickman speaking into camera. Hands open. Pleading gesture.

Content_Sketch: Week three. A hospital is hit. A school collapses. You post on social media. Six hundred retweets.
Illustration_Note: Phone screen showing tweet. Small numbers. Hospital rubble on TV behind.

Content_Sketch: Week five. You tell your people to stay home. Wait for your final call.
Illustration_Note: Stickman recording video message. Intense face. Pointing at camera.

Content_Sketch: The final call never comes. Because you don't know when to make it.
Illustration_Note: Phone in hand. Stickman staring at screen. Finger hovering over record button. Not pressing.

Content_Sketch: The regime fractures  -  but doesn't fall. Civil conflict begins to form.
Illustration_Note: Map on TV showing fragmented zones. Different colors. No clear victory.

Content_Sketch: Inside the country: some chant your name. Others call you a traitor who sold his country to foreign bombs.
Illustration_Note: Split: left  -  crowd holding old dynasty flag. Right  -  crowd burning old dynasty flag.

Content_Sketch: You stand before a Western parliament. You speak about your homeland. In a language that is not your mother tongue.
Illustration_Note: Parliament chamber. Stickman at podium. Western audience. Foreign language subtitles.

Content_Sketch: Your phone buzzes. A video from inside the country. Children lying on a hospital floor because there are no more beds.
Illustration_Note: Phone screen close-up. Hospital floor footage. Children. Stickman's face reflected in screen.

Content_Sketch: Everything you wanted for forty-seven years is happening. And it looks nothing like any dream you ever had.
Illustration_Note: Weight line. TV showing rubble. Stickman on sofa. Same position as HOOK.

Content_Sketch: Three in the morning. Your wife and daughters are asleep upstairs. You sit downstairs.
Illustration_Note: Dark suburban house. Upstairs windows dark. Downstairs TV glow. Stickman alone on sofa.

Content_Sketch: Six news screens. Split down the middle. Bombs on the left. Your interview face on the right.
Illustration_Note: Multiple TV screens. Split screen motif. War footage vs interview footage.

Content_Sketch: Your oldest daughter comes downstairs for water. She sees you. Dad are you okay. In English.
Illustration_Note: Daughter stickman in doorway. Glass of water. American pajamas. Speaking English to father.

Content_Sketch: Your three daughters speak English to each other. They use Farsi only when calling you. The language is dying inside your own family.
Illustration_Note: Three daughter stickmen talking to each other in living room. English speech. Turning to father  -  switching to Farsi.

Content_Sketch: You named your daughters as heirs. Breaking two thousand five hundred years of male succession. Because the male line died with your brother.
Illustration_Note: Photo frame: three daughters. Next to it: old photo of brother. Empty frame where succession portrait once hung.

Content_Sketch: Your house listed for eight million dollars. Rich by American standards. Homeless by your own.
Illustration_Note: Suburban mansion exterior. For sale sign. Stickman looking at house from driveway.

Content_Sketch: Every interview: Do you want to be king. You answer No. But you never drop the title.
Illustration_Note: Interview montage  -  same question repeated across multiple studios. Same answer.

Content_Sketch: You call your mother. Eighty-six. She asks: When are we going home. Same question since 1979.
Illustration_Note: Phone to ear. Stickman's face. Mother's voice implied. 47 years of the same question.

Content_Sketch: Your hands. Pilot's hands. Trained to fly combat jets. You never flew a single mission.
Illustration_Note: Close-up of stickman hands. Pilot watch. Hands that never flew combat.

Content_Sketch: Breaking news. The capital loses power completely. People light candles. Forty-seven years ago the palace burned bright every night.
Illustration_Note: Split: left  -  dark cityscape with candle dots. Right  -  memory: bright palace at night. Then/now.

Content_Sketch: You built a whole world for your children here. But you still live there  -  on the screen.
Illustration_Note: Weight line. Suburban home exterior. TV glow in window. Stickman visible through glass.

Content_Sketch: The living room. TV on mute. The capital is dark.
Illustration_Note: Mirror of HOOK. Same sofa. Same stickman. Same TV. But capital is dark now  -  no flames, just darkness.

Content_Sketch: You were seventeen when you left.
Illustration_Note: Simple text. No image except stickman silhouette.

Content_Sketch: You are sixty-five. Still here. On the sofa. In the suburb.
Illustration_Note: Stickman on sofa. Aged. Same position as seventeen-year-old who left. Time collapsed.

Content_Sketch: You don't know who you are without that country.
Illustration_Note: Weight line. Stickman face. No background. Just void.

Content_Sketch: And that country  -  doesn't know who you are anymore.
Illustration_Note: Final frame. TV turns off. Screen goes black. Stickman reflection visible in dark TV glass. Alone.
